untranslatable_reason: Signal requires macro-level indicators not in allowed list: (1) 'Share buyback activity vs. 3-year average' — no buyback or corporate action indicators available; (2) 'High-yield credit spreads' — no credit spread indicators available; (3) 'Bank lending sentiment' — no sentiment or credit condition indicators available; (4) 'Government fiscal capacity' — no macro fiscal indicators available; (5) 'Yield curve inversion' — no yield curve indicators available; (6) 'Unemployment' — no employment indicators available. Translation provided uses proxy volatility and momentum conditions but does NOT capture the regime identification logic described in the original signal.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally a MACRO REGIME IDENTIFICATION strategy requiring credit, fiscal, and employment data outside technical indicator scope. Attempted proxy: entry uses price strength (price_pos_20 > 0.5) with low volatility (hvol_20 < 0.07) to suggest risk-on regime. Exit uses rising volatility (hvol_20 > 0.06) and overbought RSI (rsi_14 > 70) to suggest regime exhaustion. However, this is a significant oversimplification. The core signal about buyback activity, HY spreads < 700bps, lending sentiment, and fiscal capacity CANNOT be accurately translated using only the allowed technical indicators. Recommend: (1) add credit spread indicators, (2) add macro sentiment indicators, or (3) accept this signal requires external data feeds.
